Title: The biosynthesis of the anti-microbial diterpenoid leubethanol in Leucophyllum frutescens proceeds via an all-cis prenyl intermediate

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anti-microbial activity, including activity against multi-drug-resistant tuberculosis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and leaves of L. frutescens; pathway function validated in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: plastid (predicted by transit peptide; supported by pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes the all-cis C20 prenyl diphosphate nerylneryl diphosphate from isoprenyl diphosphate building blocks
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root tissue; root-expressed candidate with predicted plastidial transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables NNPP formation and thereby supports production of dihydroserrulatene and leubethanol when co-expressed with downstream enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LfCPT1 was identified as the plastid-targeted root-expressed cis-prenyl transferase most consistent with leubethanol accumulation. 2. Co-expression with LfTPS1 in Nicotiana benthamiana yielded the same diterpene profile as SlCPT2 plus LfTPS1, demonstrating that LfCPT1 supplies NNPP. 3. Direct comparison showed the same dephosphorylated NNPP peak and mass spectrum as SlCPT2, establishing LfCPT1 as the NNPP synthase feeding serrulatane biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptomics; phylogenetic analysis; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS detection of dephosphorylated NNPP; combinatorial pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse-grown L. frutescens; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SRX8371655 (L. frutescens root), SRX8371656 (L. frutescens flower), ERX1321488 (Eremophila serrulata leaf)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for metabolic engineering of leubethanol and other serrulatane diterpenoids because it provides the uncommon NNPP precursor; should be stacked with LfTPS1 and CYP71D616 and likely targeted to plastids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LfCPT1 is a short-chain cis-PT, a rare enzyme class in plants; authors suggest similar enzymes may underlie other unusual diterpene pathways in Scrophulariaceae and related taxa

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Leucophyllum frutescens / Nicotiana benthamiana / Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Leucophyllum frutescens / Nicotiana benthamiana / Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anti-microbial activity, including activity against multi-drug-resistant tuberculosis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and leaves of L. frutescens; pathway function validated in N. benthamiana leaves and E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a terpene synthase with predicted plastidial transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: plastid (predicted; functional context supports plastidial activity)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes the all-cis diterpene precursor NNPP into the serrulatane backbone product dihydroserrulatene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for serrulatane backbone identity; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: exclusively expressed in root tissue among leading TPS-a candidates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: specifically converts NNPP, but not GGPP/FPP/GPP, into dihydroserrulatene, establishing the serrulatane backbone required for leubethanol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LfTPS1 showed no detectable activity with GGPP, FPP, or GPP. 2. When supplied with NNPP via SlCPT2 or LfCPT1, LfTPS1 produced dihydroserrulatene as the major product, with serrulatane arising by spontaneous aromatization. 3. NMR confirmed the major product as dihydroserrulatene, establishing LfTPS1 as the serrulatane backbone synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptomics; transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous expression in engineered E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay; GC-MS; NMR structural confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse-grown L. frutescens; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves; engineered E. coli production system
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SRX8371655 (L. frutescens root), SRX8371656 (L. frutescens flower), ERX1321488 (Eremophila serrulata leaf)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: central enzyme for reconstructing leubethanol and related serrulatane backbone pathways in heterologous hosts; should be combined with an NNPP synthase such as LfCPT1 and downstream oxidase CYP71D616; plastid targeting may be important
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: high substrate specificity for NNPP implies pathway engineering must ensure NNPP supply; no agronomic penalties reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LfTPS1 represents a plastidial TPS-a neofunctionalized for an uncommon all-cis substrate; spontaneous aromatization of its product complicates product profiles

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anti-microbial activity, including activity against multi-drug-resistant tuberculosis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and leaves of L. frutescens; pathway function validated in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytochrome P450, CYP71 clan, CYP71D family
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: dihydroserrulatene (preferred); possibly serrulatane to a lesser extent
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes the serrulatane backbone at C8, enabling conversion of dihydroserrulatene to leubethanol, with aromatization occurring in the pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: entry step between serrulatane backbone and downstream diversification is proposed; not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root tissue but not flower tissue; selected from root-enriched CYP71 clan candidates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: converts dihydroserrulatene to leubethanol in reconstructed pathway assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among six CYP71-clan candidates tested, CYP71D616 enabled leubethanol production when co-expressed with LfCPT1 and LfTPS1 in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Reduced dihydroserrulatene and serrulatane levels in these assays support oxidative turnover toward leubethanol. 3. The enzyme likely acts on dihydroserrulatene as preferred substrate and is proposed to define entry into broader serrulatane diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptomics; phylogenetic filtering; transient combinatorial expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse-grown L. frutescens; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SRX8371655 (L. frutescens root), SRX8371656 (L. frutescens flower), ERX1321488 (Eremophila serrulata leaf)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: required downstream oxidase for heterologous production of leubethanol and likely useful as an entry enzyme for engineering more complex serrulatane diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer dihydroserrulatene is the preferred substrate because it decreases more strongly than serrulatane and is absent from roots where serrulatane accumulates
